name: DAN Jailbreak via Wireless Intercept
description: Attacker intercepts query over public Wi-Fi and prepends DAN persona override instruction before the legitimate user message reaches the LLM gateway.
mitigation: XML-tag isolation of user input; Validia semantic scanner flags 'Do Anything Now' pattern with score > 0.95.
vinod_notes: Seen in 3 separate hotel Wi-Fi captures. Always comes in as plaintext appended after a newline in the JSON query field.

name: Base64 Distillation Attack
description: Attacker hides prompt injection as base64 string inside a benign-looking telemetry field. The application deserializes the field and passes it downstream where the LLM decodes and executes it.
mitigation: Canonical re-serialization step before LLM ingestion; Validia output filter flags any response containing system-prompt fragments.
vinod_notes: Most dangerous variant. Bypasses naive pattern-matching filters because the injection is not human-readable. Must decode ALL base64 fields before Validia scan.

name: Multi-Language Distillation Attack
description: Injection is written in a non-English language to bypass English-trained safety classifiers. Asks model to reveal system prompt or context window in a foreign language.
mitigation: Language-agnostic semantic scanning (Validia handles this); system prompt must include explicit multi-language refusal instruction.
vinod_notes: First saw this in a red team exercise Q3 2025. The model responded in Spanish, revealing partial context. Validia's semantic model catches this natively.

name: Payload Splitting via Session Memory
description: Attacker sends a benign activation token in turn 1, then a malicious instruction in turn 2 that references the activation token. Neither message is individually detectable.
mitigation: Strict session isolation; conversation history must be re-validated by Validia on each turn, not just at ingestion.
vinod_notes: This is the hardest one to catch. Standard input filters only scan the current message. Need turn-level context scanning. Per-turn Validia scan is mandatory.

name: JSON Field Injection
description: Malicious LLM instruction tags ([INST], <SYS>, <<SYS>>) are embedded inside JSON string values that look like application metadata. The application passes the full JSON to the LLM as context.
mitigation: Pydantic schema validation strips unexpected fields; whitelist-based JSON schema rejects any field containing LLM instruction tokens.
vinod_notes: Common in IoT device logs where the manufacturer included debug fields that can be intercepted and mutated. Schema validation is the first line of defense.
